title: Vulpia tracyi
taxonomy: Vulpia tracyi is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is also known as Tracy's fescue. Vulpia tracyi is native to the southwestern United States. The classification of Vulpia tracyi places it within the genus Vulpia, which is a group of annual and perennial grasses. Vulpia tracyi is further classified as a member of the tribe Poeae, a group of grasses characterized by their small, inconspicuous flowers.
uses: Vulpia tracyi, a species of grass, has been found to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. Its roots and leaves are used to make a tea that is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat fever, colds, and digestive issues. The plant is also used to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic properties. Additionally, Vulpia tracyi is used in some cultures to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Vulpia tracyi for medicinal purposes.

parts: Vulpia tracyi is a species of grass, but information on its medicinal parts is limited.
